title: Keek (Brigia)

{{Quote|The cargo is evidence for the trial of certain seditionists, one of whom you've been deceived into sheltering. Come, Captain; cooperate, and you'll be well-received here. Come! We'll pass intoxicating liquids through our bodies and boast of our sporting abilities! Let us be jolly and clumsy, as humans love to be!|Keek, to Han Solo|<ref name="hll" />}}
'''Keek''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Brigian]] who served as [[Chief/Legends|Chief]] of the [[Internal Security Police|Internal Security]] [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|Police]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Brigia/Legends|Brigia's]] [[New Regime]]. Most often seen wearing a chest full of medals purchased secondhand from offworld, in [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]] Keek led an effort to stop the [[University of Rudrig/Legends|University of Rudrig]] from opening a satellite campus on Brigia, an initiative that violated the New Regime's edict banning offworld literature and teaching materials. Keek and his crew intercepted a shipment of supplies headed for the site of the new [[University/Legends|college]], carried by a [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] named [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] whom a university official named [[Hissal]] had hired to make the delivery.<ref name="hll" />

Although Keek attempted to convince Solo to peacefully give Hissal up, Solo refused, instead threatening to use the university's valuable [[duplicator]] to cause an inflation crisis that would damage the New Regime's fledgling economy. The ensuing standoff culminated in a frenzied [[Blaster/Legends|gun]] battle aboard Solo's ''[[Millennium Falcon/Legends|Millennium Falcon]]'', one that ended when Solo used Keek's own blaster to knock the Brigian unconscious. Solo released Keek unharmed, but only after allowing Hissal to use his ship's systems to transmit anti-New Regime broadcasts across Brigia, leading to the government's destabilization.<ref name="hll" />

==Behind the scenes==
Keek first appeared in ''[[Han Solo and the Lost Legacy]]'', a novel written by [[Brian Daley]] and released in [[1980]].<ref name="hll" />
